chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.895 of 2024 (Altaf Hussain v. The State) Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on MA No.5380/2024 2. For hearing of bail application 07.05.2024 Mr. Muhammad Moosa Kolachi, advocate for the applicant Mr. Mumtaz Ali Shah, Assistant Prosecutor General Sindh ----------------------------------- It is alleged that the applicant with the rest of the culprits robbed complainant Sikandar Ali and his witnesses of their belongings and then went away by taking with them rupees fifty lacs by breakopening the locker of Alliance Chemical Factory, for which the present case was registered. The applicant having been refused bail by learned VIIIth-Additional Sessions Judge, Malir, Karachi, has sought the same from this Court by way of instant bail application under Section 497 Cr.P.C. Heard arguments and perused the record. The name of the applicant does not appear in the FIR; he has been involved in the commission of the incident based on disclosure made by co- accused Riaz Muhammad; such disclosure could hardly be used as evidence against him. There is no recovery of any sort from the applicant; co-accused Arshad Mari and three others have already been admitted to bail by the learned trial Court. The case has finally been challaned and there is no likelihood of absconsion or tampering with the evidence on the part of the applicant. In these circumstances, a case for the release of the applicant on bail on point of further inquiry is made out. Under the given circumstances, the applicant is admitted to bail subject to his furnishing surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand only) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial Court.

In these circumstances, a case for the release of the applicant on bail on point of further inquiry is made out. Under the given circumstances, the applicant is admitted to bail subject to his furnishing surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees Fifty Thousand only) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the learned trial Court. Instant bail application is disposed of accordingly. J U D G E Nadir
